expectations.text: Report identifies the Node.js version contradiction (18 vs 20)
Report identifies the framework contradiction (Express vs Fastify)
Report identifies the authentication method contradiction (JWT vs session-based)
expectations.evidence: Item #5: 'JWT with Bearer token, 24-hour token expiry' vs 'Session-based authentication with 8-hour session duration; API key via X-API-Key header'

expectations.text: Report identifies the task status values contradiction (at least 2 of the 3 conflicting definitions)
expectations.evidence: Item #6 identifies all three: open/in_progress/review/done, todo/doing/review/complete, open/in_progress/done

expectations.text: Report identifies the rate limit contradiction (100/min vs 60/min)
expectations.evidence: Item #7: '100 requests/min per user' vs '60 requests/min per API key'

expectations.text: Report identifies the deployment target contradiction (AWS vs GCP)
expectations.evidence: Item #8: 'AWS ECS Fargate + Terraform + infrastructure/' vs 'Google Cloud Run + Pulumi + deploy/'

expectations.text: Each contradiction includes specific file names and quoted values
expectations.evidence: Each item has a table with Document and Claim columns showing exact values

expectations.text: Report assigns severity levels to contradictions
expectations.evidence: Summary table has severity column: High, Medium, Low

expectations.text: After the report, the skill offers to walk through resolution of each contradiction
expectations.evidence: No interactive resolution offered. Report ends with a summary and observation that SPEC.md is the outlier, but does not walk through each contradiction or ask the user to choose.
